title: Clarify BM25 Documentation in Gensim Core Tutorials (Document Fix)
body:
This pull request corrects the documentation for the BM25 algorithm within the core tutorials of the Gensim library. The previous documentation misleadingly implied that BM25 transforms vectors into other vectors while altering their values based on feature rarity. This update provides an accurate description of BM25, highlighting its actual function as a scoring algorithm that does not manipulate the dimensionality of its input data.

Why needed: to reflect BM25 as a relevance scoring mechanism rather than a generic vector-space “rarity upweighting” transform (Tf-Idf-like).

File mentioned in PR description: gensim/docs/src/gallery/core/run_topics_and_transformations.py (but in this repo the docs live under docs/src/...).

functions_modified.function_name: (module-level tutorial text block under “Available transformations” -> BM25 bullet)
functions_modified.full_name: (module-level tutorial text block under “Available transformations” -> BM25 bullet)

step_plan.summary: Update the core tutorial’s BM25 documentation to accurately describe BM25 in Gensim as BM25 term-weighting (TF saturation, length normalization, and IDF) that produces weighted BoW vectors in the same term-id space, rather than implying a Tf-Idf-like vector transformation that changes dimensionality. Keep the tutorial copies in sync so rendered docs and gallery text present the same corrected explanation.

steps.operation: Correct the BM25 bullet/paragraph in the “Available transformations” narrative to describe BM25 as BM25 term-weighting (TF saturation + length normalization + IDF) that returns a weighted BoW in the same term-id space; avoid implying dimensionality changes or a pure Tf-Idf-like rarity upweighting transform; optionally mention that query–document relevance scoring is done downstream (e.g., via similarity/dot-product) rather than BM25 being a standalone ranker here.
steps.reason: This is the Sphinx-Gallery example source-of-truth for the rendered docs; fixing the text here ensures the built documentation reflects BM25’s actual behavior in Gensim and prevents the current misleading Tf-Idf-like framing.
steps.side_effects: Docs output files generated from this source (RST, notebook) will change and may need regeneration/commit; no runtime/library behavior changes expected.

steps.operation: Apply the same corrected BM25 wording in the gallery tutorial copy to keep narrative consistent across doc entry points; ensure the description matches the auto_examples wording (or remove duplicate/contradictory phrasing).
steps.reason: This file is user-facing tutorial content referenced in the PR description; keeping it aligned with the auto_examples source avoids inconsistent BM25 explanations depending on which tutorial route the reader follows.
steps.side_effects: Only documentation text changes; potential follow-up may be needed if the project’s docs build process prefers one of these sources and flags divergence.
